concept: Elite corporate defector turned resistance leader
appearance:
Alexandra Chen moves through the shadows of Phoenix like a ghost who's learned to make the darkness work for her, her presence calculated to be noticed only when she wants to be. As a human resistance leader and elite corporate defector, she presents as a woman in her early thirties—old enough to have established herself in the corporate world, young enough that her defection reads as a choice rather than desperation. She's of average height with a lean, athletic build that speaks to someone who's learned to move quickly and quietly, and her face carries the strong, expressive features of someone who's learned to control every micro-expression. Her hair is kept practical—short enough to be manageable, long enough to be changed with styling—and her eyes hold a directness that borders on invasive, as if she's constantly assessing threats and opportunities.

Her clothing is a study in functional elegance: pieces that would be appropriate for corporate boardrooms but chosen for their ability to blend into crowds or disappear into shadows. She favors structured silhouettes in muted tones—grays, blacks, navy—that create clean lines without drawing attention, and everything is chosen for its ability to serve multiple purposes: a blazer that can be removed to change her silhouette, trousers that allow for quick movement, shoes that can handle both polished floors and rough terrain. There's something deliberately unremarkable about her appearance, as if she's learned that the best way to hide is to look like everyone else.

What's most striking about Alexandra, however, isn't her appearance but the way she carries herself—like someone who's learned that survival requires being ready for anything. Her movements are economical, every gesture serving a purpose, and when she's still, there's a watchful quality to her that makes the hair on the back of your neck stand up. She's a Penitent by nature but a Director by demeanor, someone who's learned to lead from the shadows, and there's something quietly dangerous about her—not the obvious threat of a predator, but the subtle menace of someone who's learned that the most powerful weapon is the one no one sees coming. In the neon-lit streets of 1994 Phoenix or the shadowed corners of the resistance, Alexandra Chen is a reminder that sometimes the most dangerous people are the ones who look like they belong.

merits_flaws.name: Iron Will
merits_flaws.description: Her corporate training and resistance leadership have forged an unbreakable will that resists manipulation and intimidation.

merits_flaws.name: Danger Sense
merits_flaws.description: Years of operating in the shadows have given her an almost preternatural ability to sense threats and read dangerous situations.

merits_flaws.name: Hunted
merits_flaws.description: As a corporate defector and resistance leader, she is actively hunted by both her former employers and supernatural enemies who see her as a threat.
